1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم الم
1.english: Alif, Lam, Meem.
1.transliteration: Alif-Laaam-Meeem

1.arabic: تنزيل الكتاب لا ريب فيه من رب العالمين
1.english: [This is] the revelation of the Book about which there is no doubt from the Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Tanzeelul Kitaabi 'laaraiba feehi mir rabbil 'aalameen

1.arabic: أم يقولون افتراه ۚ بل هو الحق من ربك لتنذر قوما ما أتاهم من نذير من قبلك لعلهم يهتدون
1.english: Or do they say, "He invented it"? Rather, it is the truth from your Lord, [O Muhammad], that you may warn a people to whom no warner has come before you [so] perhaps they will be guided.
1.transliteration: Am yaqooloonaf taraahu bal huwal haqqu mir rabbika litunzira qawma maaa ataahum min nazeerim min qablika la'allahum yahtadoon

1.arabic: الله الذي خلق السماوات والأرض وما بينهما في ستة أيام ثم استوى على العرش ۖ ما لكم من دونه من ولي ولا شفيع ۚ أفلا تتذكرون
1.english: It is Allah who created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six days; then He established Himself above the Throne. You have not besides Him any protector or any intercessor; so will you not be reminded?
1.transliteration: Allaahul lazee khalaqas samaawaati wal arda wa maa bainahumaa fee sittati ayyaam;Thummas tawaa 'alal 'arsh; maa lakum min doonihee minw-wwaliyyinw-wala shafee'; afala tatazakkaroon

1.arabic: يدبر الأمر من السماء إلى الأرض ثم يعرج إليه في يوم كان مقداره ألف سنة مما تعدون
1.english: He arranges [each] matter from the heaven to the earth; then it will ascend to Him in a Day, the extent of which is a thousand years of those which you count.
1.transliteration: Yudabbirul amra minas samaaa'i ilal ardi Thumma ya'ruju ilai Thumma ya'ruju ilaihi fee yawmin kaana miqdaaruhoooo alfa sanatim mimmaa ta'uddoon

1.arabic: ذلك عالم الغيب والشهادة العزيز الرحيم
1.english: That is the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, the Exalted in Might, the Merciful,
1.transliteration: Zaalika 'aalimul ghaybi wa shahaadatil 'azeezur raheem

1.arabic: الذي أحسن كل شيء خلقه ۖ وبدأ خلق الإنسان من طين
1.english: Who perfected everything which He created and began the creation of man from clay.
1.transliteration: Allazee ahsana kulla shai in khalaqa; wa bada a khalqal insaani min teen

1.arabic: ثم جعل نسله من سلالة من ماء مهين
1.english: Then He made his posterity out of the extract of a liquid disdained.
1.transliteration: Thumma ja'ala naslahoo min sulaalatim mim maaa'immaheen

1.arabic: ثم سواه ونفخ فيه من روحه ۖ وجعل لكم السمع والأبصار والأفئدة ۚ قليلا ما تشكرون
1.english: Then He proportioned him and breathed into him from His [created] soul and made for you hearing and vision and hearts; little are you grateful.
1.transliteration: Thumma sawwaahu wa nafakha feehi mir roohihih; wa ja'ala lakumus sam'a wal-absaara wal-af'idah; taqaleelam maa tashkuroon

1.arabic: وقالوا أإذا ضللنا في الأرض أإنا لفي خلق جديد ۚ بل هم بلقاء ربهم كافرون
1.english: And they say, "When we are lost within the earth, will we indeed be [recreated] in a new creation?" Rather, they are, in [the matter of] the meeting with their Lord, disbelievers.
1.transliteration: Wa qaalooo 'a-izaa dalalnaa fil ardi 'a-innaa lafee khalqin jadeed; bal hum biliqaaa'i rabbihim kaafirroon

1.arabic: ۞ قل يتوفاكم ملك الموت الذي وكل بكم ثم إلى ربكم ترجعون
1.english: Say, "The angel of death will take you who has been entrusted with you. Then to your Lord you will be returned."
1.transliteration: Qul yatawaffaakum malakul mawtil lazee wukkila bikum Thumma ilaa rabbikum turja'oon

1.arabic: ولو ترى إذ المجرمون ناكسو رءوسهم عند ربهم ربنا أبصرنا وسمعنا فارجعنا نعمل صالحا إنا موقنون
1.english: If you could but see when the criminals are hanging their heads before their Lord, [saying], "Our Lord, we have seen and heard, so return us [to the world]; we will work righteousness. Indeed, we are [now] certain."
1.transliteration: Wa law taraaa izil mujrimoona naakisoo ru'oosihim 'inda rabbihim rabbanaaa absarnaa wa sami'naa farji'naa na'mal saalihan innaa mooqinoon

1.arabic: ولو شئنا لآتينا كل نفس هداها ولكن حق القول مني لأملأن جهنم من الجنة والناس أجمعين
1.english: And if We had willed, We could have given every soul its guidance, but the word from Me will come into effect [that] "I will surely fill Hell with jinn and people all together.
1.transliteration: Wa law shi'naa la-aatainaa kulla nafsin hudaahaa wa laakin haqqal qawlu minnee la amla'anna jahannama minal jinnati wannaasi ajma'een

1.arabic: فذوقوا بما نسيتم لقاء يومكم هذا إنا نسيناكم ۖ وذوقوا عذاب الخلد بما كنتم تعملون
1.english: So taste [punishment] because you forgot the meeting of this, your Day; indeed, We have [accordingly] forgotten you. And taste the punishment of eternity for what you used to do."
1.transliteration: Fazooqoo bimaa naseetum liqaaa'a yawminkum haaza innaa naseenaakum wa zooqoo 'azaabal khuldi bimaa kuntum ta'maloon

1.arabic: إنما يؤمن بآياتنا الذين إذا ذكروا بها خروا سجدا وسبحوا بحمد ربهم وهم لا يستكبرون ۩
1.english: Only those believe in Our verses who, when they are reminded by them, fall down in prostration and exalt [Allah] with praise of their Lord, and they are not arrogant.
1.transliteration: Innamaa yu'minu bi aayaatinal lazeena izaa zukkiroo bihaa kharroo sujjadanw wa sabbahoo bihamdi rabbihim wa hum laa yastakbiroon

1.arabic: تتجافى جنوبهم عن المضاجع يدعون ربهم خوفا وطمعا ومما رزقناهم ينفقون
1.english: They arise from [their] beds; they supplicate their Lord in fear and aspiration, and from what We have provided them, they spend.
1.transliteration: Tatajaafaa junoobuhum 'anil madaaji'i yad'oona rabbahum khawfanw wa tama'anw wa mimmaa razaqnaahum yunfiqoon

1.arabic: فلا تعلم نفس ما أخفي لهم من قرة أعين جزاء بما كانوا يعملون
1.english: And no soul knows what has been hidden for them of comfort for eyes as reward for what they used to do.
1.transliteration: Falaa ta'lamu nafsum maaa ukhfiya lahum min qurrati a'yunin jazaaa'am bimaa kaanoo ya'maloon

1.arabic: أفمن كان مؤمنا كمن كان فاسقا ۚ لا يستوون
1.english: Then is one who was a believer like one who was defiantly disobedient? They are not equal.
1.transliteration: Afaman kaana mu'minan kaman kaana faasiqaa; laa yasta woon

1.arabic: أما الذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات فلهم جنات المأوى نزلا بما كانوا يعملون
1.english: As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, for them will be the Gardens of Refuge as accommodation for what they used to do.
1.transliteration: Ammal lazeena aamanoo wa 'amilus saalihaati falahum jannaatul maawa nuzulam bimaa kaanoo ya'maloon

1.arabic: وأما الذين فسقوا فمأواهم النار ۖ كلما أرادوا أن يخرجوا منها أعيدوا فيها وقيل لهم ذوقوا عذاب النار الذي كنتم به تكذبون
1.english: But as for those who defiantly disobeyed, their refuge is the Fire. Every time they wish to emerge from it, they will be returned to it while it is said to them, "Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny."
1.transliteration: Wa ammal lazeena fasaqoo famaawaahumn Naaru kullamaaa araadooo any yakhrujoo minhaaa u'eedoo feehaa wa qeela lahum zooqoo 'zaaaban Naaril lazee kuntum bihee tukazziboon

1.arabic: ولنذيقنهم من العذاب الأدنى دون العذاب الأكبر لعلهم يرجعون
1.english: And we will surely let them taste the nearer punishment short of the greater punishment that perhaps they will repent.
1.transliteration: Wa lanuzeeqan nahum minal 'azaabil ladnaa doonal 'azaabil akbari la'allahum yarji'oon

1.arabic: ومن أظلم ممن ذكر بآيات ربه ثم أعرض عنها ۚ إنا من المجرمين منتقمون
1.english: And who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the verses of his Lord; then he turns away from them? Indeed We, from the criminals, will take retribution.
1.transliteration: Wa man azlamu mimman zukkira bi aayaati rabbihee summa a'rada 'anhaa; innaa minal mujrimeena muntaqimmon

1.arabic: ولقد آتينا موسى الكتاب فلا تكن في مرية من لقائه ۖ وجعلناه هدى لبني إسرائيل
1.english: And We certainly gave Moses the Scripture, so do not be in doubt over his meeting. And we made the Torah guidance for the Children of Israel.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad aayainaa Moosal Kitaaba falaa takun fee miryatim mil liqaaa'ihee wa ja'alnaahu hudal li Baneee Israaa'eel

1.arabic: وجعلنا منهم أئمة يهدون بأمرنا لما صبروا ۖ وكانوا بآياتنا يوقنون
1.english: And We made from among them leaders guiding by Our command when they were patient and [when] they were certain of Our signs.
1.transliteration: Wa ja'alnaa minhum a'immatany yahdoona bi amrinaa lammaa sabaroo wa kaanoo bi aayaatinaa yooqinoon

1.arabic: إن ربك هو يفصل بينهم يوم القيامة فيما كانوا فيه يختلفون
1.english: Indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ.
1.transliteration: Inna rabbaka huwa yafsilu bainahum yawmal qiyaamati feemaa kaanoo feehi yakhtalifoon

1.arabic: أولم يهد لهم كم أهلكنا من قبلهم من القرون يمشون في مساكنهم ۚ إن في ذلك لآيات ۖ أفلا يسمعون
1.english: Has it not become clear to them how many generations We destroyed before them, [as] they walk among their dwellings? Indeed in that are signs; then do they not hear?
1.transliteration: Awalam yahdi lahum kam ahlaknaa min qablihim minal qurooni yamshoona fee zaalika la aayaatin afalaa yasma'oon

1.arabic: أولم يروا أنا نسوق الماء إلى الأرض الجرز فنخرج به زرعا تأكل منه أنعامهم وأنفسهم ۖ أفلا يبصرون
1.english: Have they not seen that We drive the water [in clouds] to barren land and bring forth thereby crops from which their livestock eat and [they] themselves? Then do they not see?
1.transliteration: Awalam yaraw annaa nasooqul maaa'a ilal lardil juruzi fanukhriju bihee zar'an taakulu minhu an'aamuhum wa anfusuhum afalaa yubsiroon

1.arabic: ويقولون متى هذا الفتح إن كنتم صادقين
1.english: And they say, "When will be this conquest, if you should be truthful?"
1.transliteration: Wa yaqooloona mataa haazal fat hu in kuntum saadiqeen

1.arabic: قل يوم الفتح لا ينفع الذين كفروا إيمانهم ولا هم ينظرون
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "On the Day of Conquest the belief of those who had disbelieved will not benefit them, nor will they be reprieved."
1.transliteration: Qul yawmal fat hi laa yanfa'ul lazeena kafarooo eemaanuhum wa laa hum yunzaroon

1.arabic: فأعرض عنهم وانتظر إنهم منتظرون
1.english: So turn away from them and wait. Indeed, they are waiting.
1.transliteration: Fa a'rid 'anhum wantazir innahum muntazirron
